name: Acorn Squash with Wehani Rice & Pecan Stuffing
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Wehani rice or rice blend
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the squash lengthwise and remove the seeds.
2. Steam for 20 minutes (the squash will not be fully cooked). Set aside.
- This step can be completed one day ahead; refrigerate the squash.
3. Bring water and tamari to a boil in a 2-quart saucepan over medium-high heat.
4. Add rice and return to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
- Rice may be cooked up to 2 days ahead; refrigerate until ready to use.
5. For the stuffing:
- In a large skillet, sauté carrots, celery, onion, thyme, and ginger in margarine or butter until onions are golden.
- Thoroughly toss in pecans, orange zest, and rice. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
- Remove from heat and set aside.
- Stuffing may be prepared 1 day ahead and refrigerated.
6. Put glaze ingredients into a small jar and shake vigorously to combine. Keep refrigerated until ready to use.
- Glaze may be prepared up to 1 day ahead.
7. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) one hour before serving.
8. Mound stuffing mixture into each squash half, about 2 inches over the top of the squash.
9. Place stuffed squash halves in a baking pan filled with 1/2 cup of water.
10. Drizzle some glaze over the stuffing and brush onto the squash. Cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes.
11. Drizzle remaining glaze over the squash, and continue baking uncovered for another 20 minutes until glazed and lightly browned.
12. Serve immediately.
Calories per serving: 326
Grams of fat: 5% of daily value
Fat calories: 14%
Cholesterol: 0 mg
Grams of fiber: 5.8
